LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, LINDA CENTORE, DENLY HERBERT, NELSON ARTIGA, PERLINE AKHAVAN AND ANASTASIOS KARYDIS' DEMURRER. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE COURT MAY NOT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE REQUESTED RECORDS. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED A LACK OF CONSENT; SUSTAINED AS TO EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM; SUSTAINED AS TO TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON SHOEMAKER V FRIEDBERG (1947) 80 CAL APP 2D 911, 915-916; OVERRULED AS TO TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 1685 FOR ALLEGED GROSS NEGLIGENCE AND SUSTAINED AS TO THIRTEENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LACK OF FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM, FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT DID NOT GRANT LEAVE TO ADD ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTIONS. FURTHER AS TO THE 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR MEDICAL BATTERY UNDER COBBS V. GRANT (1972) 8 CAL. 3D 299, 239. PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO THE PROCEDURE AND WAS CONSCIOUS OF BEING GIVEN INJECTIONS. THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES DO NOT ALLEGE EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AS CONTRASTED A CLAIM FOR DENTAL MALPRACTICE. THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PRIVILEGED UNDER SHOEMAKER V. FRIEDBERG (1947) 80 CAL. APP. 2D 911, 915-916. THE 14TH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2008 Continued To Oct-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Julie Martucci of Hassard Bonnington (288.9800) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 8th cause of action, the Court did not grant leave to add additional causes of actions. Further as to the 7th and 8th causes of action, the facts alleged do not support a claim for medical battery under Cobbs v. Grant (1972) 8 Cal. 3d 299, 239. Plaintiff consented to the procedure and was conscious of being given injections. The 9th and 10th causes do not allege extreme and outrageous conduct as contrasted a claim for dental malpractice. The facts alleged in the 11th cause of action are privileged under Shoemaker v. Friedberg (1947) 80 Cal. App. 2d 911, 915-916. The 14th cause of action fails to allege a cause of action for intentional misrepresentation. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
